
My research pays rigorous attention to research design, case selection and comparative case studies, and uses qualitative methods such as ethnographic fieldwork, in-depth interviews and participant observation, in addition to using the quantitative toolkit such as large-n data analysis. These research themes and methodological approaches form the basis of the courses I teach [Inro to Comparative Politics, Politics of India/South Asia, Globalization and its Critics, Senior Seminar on Comparative Ethnic Politics, and Qualitative Methods].
